title: ‘Brilliant, unique soul’: Mother remembers Fall River teen who drowned in pond
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: Tristan Major, a 17-year-old from Fall River, drowned in South Watuppa Pond on July 12, authorities confirmed.
His mother remembers him as a "brilliant, unique soul" aiming to start a therapeutic horse ranch with his family.
Fall River Public Schools made counselors available to support students after the tragedy involving the former student.

title: Jennifer Garner posts throwback video of karaoke night at Nantucket restaurant
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Jennifer Garner shared a throwback video of a karaoke night at Gaslight in Nantucket, recorded on September 30, 2025.
The video features Garner singing ABBA's 'Dancing Queen' with co-stars and Nantucket guide Bill Jamieson.
Garner reminisced about filming 'The Five-Star Weekend' and highlighted Nantucket's attractions in recent interviews.

title: Here’s what meteorologists are saying about the yellow skies across the region
author: Jack Pickell
bullets: Wildfire smoke from Canada caused yellow skies over Boston and is expected to persist for another day.
The smoke, combined with clouds, has limited the forecasted heat, according to the National Weather Service.
Meteorologists note potential for increased smoke concentration, impacting air quality and temperatures with hazy conditions.

title: A very special traffic cone arrives in Boston, courtesy of our new friends in Glasgow
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: A unique traffic cone from Glasgow arrived in Boston to symbolize the new sister city relationship with Glasgow.
The cone, signed by Glasgow Chamber CEO, will raise funds for mental health charities in Massachusetts and Scotland.
Massachusetts Gov. Maura Healey and Boston Mayor Michelle Wu are set to officially welcome the cone at Logan Airport.

title: 3 more MBTA employees charged with overtime fraud, falsifying track inspections
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Three MBTA employees were charged with overtime fraud and falsifying track inspections at the Cabot Yard.
Four former MBTA employees face new conspiracy charges related to falsified inspection reports and overtime fraud.
The charges allege employees worked on private vehicles during duty and falsified timesheets for unworked shifts.

title: The World Cup was great, but Boston still doesn’t need the Olympics
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: France won the final World Cup game held in Foxborough, concluding Boston's involvement in the tournament.
Hosting the World Cup cost Massachusetts taxpayers nearly $100 million, mainly for transportation improvements.
Boston's past Olympic bid would have cost an estimated $4.7 billion, far exceeding World Cup expenses.

title: Bill Belichick’s daughter-in-law wanted for arrest in North Carolina after traffic stop
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: A North Carolina judge issued an arrest order for Jen Belichick after she missed a court date for speeding.
Jennifer Schmitt Belichick, married to Steve Belichick, was cited for driving 88 mph in a 70 mph zone.
Bill Belichick, now UNC head coach, was previously with the Patriots; Steve is UNC's defensive coordinator.

title: Karen Read, Turtleboy look to get witnesses’ defamation suit tossed
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Karen Read and Aidan Kearney moved to dismiss a defamation lawsuit filed by witnesses from Read's murder case.
The witnesses allege defamation and harassment after Read and Kearney implicated them in John O'Keefe's 2022 death.
Read and Kearney invoked the anti-SLAPP law, claiming protection for actions aimed at publicizing a cover-up allegation.

title: The invisible force helping fuel this summer’s intense heat, and all our weather
author: David Epstein
bullets: The jet stream's movements are crucial in creating global weather patterns, including recent intense heat waves.
Quasi-resonant amplification may cause weather extremes, but remains unproven by current scientific models.
Heat advisories span from Montana to Maine due to a single wavy loop of the jet stream.

title: Patriots 2026 position preview: Offensive line edition
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Patriots' offensive line showed improvements in 2025 but remains a key focus heading into the 2026 season.
Jared Wilson moves to center, Alijah Vera-Tucker joins as guard, and Will Campbell recovers from an MCL injury.
The Patriots' line allowed 21 sacks in the postseason but ranked sixth in pass-block efficiency in the regular season.
